name: Puducherry
tagline: The French Riviera of the East

places.name: Auroville
places.description: An experimental, universal township dedicated to the ideal of human unity. It was founded in 1968 by Mirra Alfassa (The Mother) and designed by architect Roger Anger.
places.history: Designed to be a place where men and women of all countries are able to live in peace and progressive harmony above all creeds.
places.famousFor: Matrimandir, sustainable living, and universal peace.

nearbyAttractions.name: Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges
Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus

places.name: Matrimandir
places.description: The spiritual and physical center of Auroville. It is a massive, stunning golden metallic sphere designed as a place for silent, individual concentration.
places.history: Construction took 37 years to complete. It does not belong to any religion or sect.
places.famousFor: Golden dome, silent meditation chamber, and peace.

nearbyAttractions.name: Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges
Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus

places.name: Sri Aurobindo Ashram
places.description: A highly revered spiritual community founded by the Indian freedom fighter and philosopher, Sri Aurobindo, in 1926. It is one of the most visited ashrams in India.
places.history: Sri Aurobindo retreated here after leaving politics, focusing entirely on spiritual evolution and 'Integral Yoga'.
places.famousFor: Samadhi (tomb) of Sri Aurobindo and The Mother.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Promenade Beach (Rock Beach)
places.description: A deeply scenic, 1.2-kilometer long rocky beachfront stretching along the vibrant French Quarter. It is entirely pedestrianized every evening.
places.history: Features historic landmarks including the French War Memorial, a towering statue of Mahatma Gandhi, and the old lighthouse.
places.famousFor: Evening strolls, cafes, and Gandhi statue.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Paradise Beach
places.description: An incredibly pristine, isolated beach boasting ultra-soft golden sand and clear blue waters. It is accessible only via a scenic boat ride through the Chunnambar backwaters.
places.history: Maintained exceptionally well by the tourism department, ensuring it remains one of the cleanest beaches in South India.
places.famousFor: Pristine sands, boating, and beach volleyball.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
places.description: A magnificent Gothic architecture church, striking with its bold red and white facade and stunning, incredibly rare stained glass panels depicting the life of Christ.
places.history: Consecrated in 1907. It is the only basilica in Puducherry and a deeply significant pilgrimage center.
places.famousFor: Gothic architecture, stained glass, and peaceful ambiance.

nearbyAttractions.name: Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Serenity Beach
places.description: True to its name, a quiet and picturesque beach framed by palm trees. It is particularly famous among the surfing community for its excellent waves.
places.history: The local surfing community established surfing schools here, transforming it into a hub for water sports.
places.famousFor: Surfing, peaceful sunrise, and fishing boats.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Arikamedu Archaeological Site
places.description: The fascinating ruins of an ancient Roman trade center. Excavations have unearthed massive brick structures, Roman coins, and ancient pottery dating back to 2 BCE.
places.history: Discovered by archaeologists including Sir Mortimer Wheeler. It proves extensive maritime trade existed between ancient Rome and Tamil Nadu.
places.famousFor: Ancient ruins, Roman history, and peaceful riverside.

nearbyAttractions.name: Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges
Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus

places.name: Ousteri Lake (Osudu Lake)
places.description: The largest freshwater lake in Puducherry, recognized as an IUCN designated wetland. It is a critical sanctuary for numerous migratory bird species.
places.history: Man-made lake constructed a century ago. It acts as the primary freshwater source and an ecological lifeline for the region.
places.famousFor: Bird watching, boating, and scenic sunsets.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Karaikal Beach
places.description: One of the finest and cleanest natural beaches in South India. It features a wide beachfront, calm waters perfect for swimming, and extensive boating facilities on the Arasalar River.
places.history: Developed heavily by the local tourism department to feature a scenic 2km road along the river leading to the beach.
places.famousFor: Clean sands, river boating, and beautiful sunrises.

places.name: Sri Dharbaranyeswarar Temple
places.description: An incredibly famous and powerful Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Saturn (Saneeswarar). Millions of pilgrims visit this shrine seeking relief from astrological afflictions.
places.history: According to legend, King Nala was relieved of his afflictions by Lord Saneeswarar here. It is one of the Navagraha temples.
places.famousFor: Lord Saneeswarar shrine, Nala Theertham holy tank.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: French War Memorial
places.description: An elegant and somber memorial standing on the Promenade Beach, dedicated to the brave soldiers of French India who sacrificed their lives during World War I.
places.history: Built in 1971. Every year on Bastille Day (July 14), it is beautifully illuminated to honor the fallen.
places.famousFor: Bastille Day illuminations, colonial architecture.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Chunnambar Boat House
places.description: A deeply scenic backwater boating facility that serves as the exclusive gateway to the pristine Paradise Beach. It is surrounded by thick mangrove forests.
places.history: Operated by the Puducherry Tourism Development Corporation, it offers tree houses and riverside accommodations.
places.famousFor: Ferry to Paradise Beach, backwater boating, and treehouses.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Bharathi Park
places.description: A lush, well-maintained green park located at the exact center of the French Quarter, featuring the stunning Aayi Mandapam monument at its core.
places.history: The Aayi Mandapam was built in the 19th century by Napoleon III to honor Aayi, a local courtesan who demolished her house to supply water to the city.
places.famousFor: Aayi Mandapam, peaceful walking paths, and heritage buildings.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Immaculate Conception Cathedral
places.description: The mother church of the Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Pondicherry and Cuddalore. This massive cathedral boasts stunning Portuguese and French architectural elements.
places.history: Built in 1791 over the ruins of three older churches that were repeatedly destroyed during the colonial wars between the French, British, and Dutch.
places.famousFor: Colonial architecture, grand altar, and historical significance.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Sri Manakula Vinayagar Temple
places.description: An incredibly historic Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Ganesha, existing since before the French arrived in Puducherry. The temple is famous for its golden chariot and the temple elephant, Lakshmi.
places.history: Surviving multiple attempts at destruction during the colonial era, it remains one of the most culturally significant sites in the French Quarter.
places.famousFor: Golden chariot, ancient idol, and intricate ceiling murals.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges
places.description: A stunning Greco-Roman style church that is the only one in Puducherry to offer mass in three languages: French, Tamil, and English. It closely resembles the Basilica at Lourdes in France.
places.history: Constructed in 1855 by Napoleon III. It is famous for its smooth limestone interior mixed with the whites of eggs.
places.famousFor: Greco-Roman architecture, Joan of Arc statue.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus

places.name: Puducherry Museum
places.description: Housed in a beautiful heritage building, this museum boasts an incredible collection of ancient artifacts from the Arikamedu Roman settlement, Chola bronzes, and French colonial furniture.
places.history: It serves as the definitive guardian of the region's diverse history, from prehistoric times through to the French colonial period.
places.famousFor: Roman artifacts, ancient bronze sculptures.

nearbyAttractions.name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges

places.name: Mahe Beach
places.description: Located in the Mahe district of Puducherry (geographically situated in Kerala), this pristine and relatively unknown beach offers stunning sunset views over the Arabian Sea.
places.history: Mahe was a former French colony on the Malabar coast, maintaining its distinct French-Malayali cultural blend.
places.famousFor: Arabian sea sunsets, palm-fringed coastline, fishing boats.

places.name: Yanam River Walk
places.description: A beautifully landscaped promenade along the Gauthami Godavari River in the Yanam enclave of Puducherry. It features a massive replica of the Eiffel Tower.
places.history: Yanam is a former French colony surrounded geographically by Andhra Pradesh, retaining a unique Franco-Telugu heritage.
places.famousFor: Eiffel Tower replica, river boating, and serene evenings.

places.name: Sri Karneshwar Nataraja Temple
places.description: An incredibly unique and striking temple built entirely in the shape of a massive Egyptian pyramid. It is dedicated to Lord Shiva in his Nataraja form.
places.history: Constructed relatively recently with the support of the Auroville community, blending Indian spirituality with global architectural geometry.
places.famousFor: Pyramid architecture, beachfront location, and meditation.

nearbyAttractions.name: Eglise de Notre Dame des Anges
Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
